indicators.fullTitle: Capture fisheries production (metric tons)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Capture fisheries production adds coastal, river, and ocean-resource geography to the agriculture catalog.

indicators.fullTitle: Carbon dioxide (CO2) net fluxes from LULUCF - Deforestation (Mt CO2e)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Deforestation CO2 flux gives players a land-use climate signal distinct from energy emissions.

indicators.fullTitle: Carbon dioxide (CO2) net fluxes from LULUCF - Forest Land (Mt CO2e)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Forest land CO2 flux highlights where forests act as carbon sources or sinks, not simply where forests exist.

indicators.fullTitle: Taxes on income, profits and capital gains (% of total taxes)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Taxes on income, profits, and capital gains as a share of revenue reveal different fiscal structures than total tax take.

indicators.fullTitle: Travel services (% of service imports, BoP)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Travel services as a share of service imports can reveal outbound tourism, business travel, and small-market service dependence.

indicators.fullTitle: Merchandise imports from low- and middle-income economies within region (% of total merchandise imports)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Intra-regional imports highlight trade blocs, neighbors, and distance in a different way than export-side regional trade.

indicators.fullTitle: Insurance and financial services (% of service imports, BoP)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Financial service imports add a specialized services-trade puzzle for advanced players.

indicators.fullTitle: Broad money (% of GDP)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Broad money as a share of GDP gives an expert signal of financial depth and monetary structure.

indicators.fullTitle: Bank nonperforming loans to total gross loans (%)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Nonperforming loans can expose banking stress and credit cycles that ordinary income maps miss.

indicators.fullTitle: Bank capital to assets ratio (%)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Bank capital to assets adds a financial-system resilience clue for advanced economy rounds.

indicators.fullTitle: External balance on goods and services (% of GDP)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: External balance shows exporter and importer positions without requiring players to read separate export and import maps.

indicators.fullTitle: Final consumption expenditure (% of GDP)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Final consumption as a share of GDP helps distinguish domestic-demand economies from investment- or export-heavy ones.

indicators.fullTitle: Energy intensity level of primary energy (MJ/$2021 PPP GDP)
indicators.unit: megajoules per 2017 PPP dollar of GDP
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Energy intensity separates heavy industry, climate, and efficiency patterns from simple electricity access maps.

indicators.fullTitle: Energy imports, net (% of energy use)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Net energy imports reveal import dependence and resource-exporter contrasts in a compact choropleth.

indicators.fullTitle: Electric power transmission and distribution losses (% of output)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Electricity losses are an intuitive infrastructure-efficiency clue that can differ from access or generation mix.

indicators.fullTitle: Alternative and nuclear energy (% of total energy use)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Alternative and nuclear energy highlights nuclear, hydro, geothermal, and other non-fossil energy systems outside electricity-only shares.

indicators.fullTitle: Combustible renewables and waste (% of total energy)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Combustible renewables and waste reveal household biomass and traditional energy patterns that electricity maps can hide.

indicators.fullTitle: GDP per unit of energy use (constant 2021 PPP $ per kg of oil equivalent)
indicators.unit: PPP dollars per kilogram of oil equivalent
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Energy productivity gives expert players a complementary view to energy intensity and industrial structure.

indicators.fullTitle: Carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions (total) excluding LULUCF (% change from 1990)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: CO2 emissions change rewards players who notice industrial growth, transition economies, and decarbonization patterns.

indicators.fullTitle: Methane (CH4) emissions (total) excluding LULUCF (% change from 1990)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Methane emissions change adds an agriculture, waste, and energy clue that does not exactly mirror CO2.

indicators.fullTitle: Total greenhouse gas emissions excluding LULUCF (% change from 1990)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Total greenhouse gas change combines energy, land, agriculture, and industrial shifts into a challenging expert map.

indicators.fullTitle: Adjusted savings: carbon dioxide damage (% of GNI)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: CO2 damage share converts emissions into an economic intensity clue that can differ from raw emission totals.

indicators.fullTitle: Adjusted savings: energy depletion (% of GNI)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Energy depletion share highlights oil, gas, and coal extraction dependence without using raw production totals.

indicators.fullTitle: Adjusted savings: mineral depletion (% of GNI)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Mineral depletion share adds a resource-economy clue for mining-dependent countries.

indicators.fullTitle: Mineral rents (% of GDP)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Mineral rents are a direct but still puzzly map of mining dependence and export specialization.

indicators.fullTitle: Coal rents (% of GDP)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Coal rents isolate coal-producing economies more cleanly than broader fossil-fuel indicators.

indicators.fullTitle: Fish species, threatened
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Threatened fish species can reveal coastal, river-basin, and biodiversity-hotspot geography.

indicators.fullTitle: Bird species, threatened
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Threatened bird species are a distinctive biodiversity puzzle with island and tropical signals.

indicators.fullTitle: Mammal species, threatened
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Threatened mammal species add an animal-habitat geography clue that differs from forest or protected-area shares.

indicators.fullTitle: Plant species (higher), threatened
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Threatened plant species highlight biodiversity hotspots and island endemism in an expert-friendly way.

indicators.fullTitle: Mortality from CVD, cancer, diabetes or CRD between exact ages 30 and 70 (%)
indicators.unit: percent probability of dying between ages 30 and 70
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Premature mortality from noncommunicable diseases creates a fair health-transition puzzle with broad global coverage.

indicators.fullTitle: Incidence of HIV, ages 15-49 (per 1,000 uninfected population ages 15-49)
indicators.unit: new infections per 1,000 uninfected population ages 15-49
indicators.whyMysteryMap: HIV incidence is a distinct and geographically meaningful expert health map with clear regional signals.

indicators.fullTitle: Antiretroviral therapy coverage (% of people living with HIV)
indicators.unit: percent of people living with HIV
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Antiretroviral therapy coverage offers a health-system access clue that is different from incidence or mortality.

indicators.fullTitle: UHC service coverage index
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Universal health coverage service coverage gives a broad, readable health-system map that is not just spending or income.

indicators.fullTitle: Mortality rate attributed to household and ambient air pollution, age-standardized (per 100,000 population)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Air pollution mortality combines environment, urbanization, fuel use, and health into a strong expert puzzle.

indicators.fullTitle: Tuberculosis treatment success rate (% of new cases)
indicators.unit: percent of new and relapse cases
indicators.whyMysteryMap: TB treatment success adds a health-system performance clue that differs from raw disease burden.

indicators.fullTitle: Tuberculosis case detection rate (%, all forms)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: TB case detection turns disease surveillance and health access into a challenging but fair map.

indicators.fullTitle: Population ages 15-64 (% of total population)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Working-age population share gives a readable demographic clue distinct from fertility, youth, and elderly-share maps.

indicators.fullTitle: Adjusted savings: education expenditure (% of GNI)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Education expenditure as a share of GNI adds a compact investment-priority map beyond enrollment outcomes.

indicators.fullTitle: Human capital index plus (HCI+): overall score, total (scale 0–325)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: The Human Capital Index blends health and education outcomes into a broad but still geographically interesting development puzzle.

indicators.fullTitle: Human capital index plus (HCI+): education pillar score, total (scale 0–188)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: The education component of human capital gives advanced players a compact education-quality signal beyond enrollment.

indicators.fullTitle: Adjusted net savings, including particulate emission damage (% of GNI)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Adjusted net savings combines investment, depletion, and environmental damage into a difficult development-sustainability clue.

indicators.fullTitle: Account ownership at a financial institution or with a mobile-money-service provider, poorest 40% (% of population ages 15+)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Account ownership among the poorest 40 percent reveals inclusion patterns that average finance maps can hide.

indicators.fullTitle: Account ownership at a financial institution or with a mobile-money-service provider, young adults (% of population ages 15-24)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Young-adult account ownership adds a generational financial-inclusion puzzle with a clear unit.

indicators.fullTitle: Merchandise trade (% of GDP)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Merchandise trade as a share of GDP is a fair, high-coverage map of openness, hubs, islands, and resource trade.

indicators.fullTitle: Travel services (% of service exports, BoP)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Travel services as a share of service exports makes tourism-dependent economies stand out without raw visitor totals.

indicators.fullTitle: Merchandise exports to high-income economies (% of total merchandise exports)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Exports to high-income economies reward reasoning about trade corridors, neighbors, and market orientation.

indicators.fullTitle: Merchandise imports from high-income economies (% of total merchandise imports)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Imports from high-income economies add a complementary trade-partner pattern to export-side maps.

indicators.fullTitle: Merchandise exports to low- and middle-income economies within region (% of total merchandise exports)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: Intra-regional exports highlight regional integration, distance, and trade-bloc geography in a compact form.

indicators.fullTitle: Automated teller machines (ATMs) (per 100,000 adults)
indicators.whyMysteryMap: ATM density gives players a tangible connectivity-and-finance clue that differs from mobile or internet access.

sameConceptFindings.finding: New working-age-share is very close to the inverse of existing age-dependency (Pearson r = -0.990 over 169 countries). It has a simpler unit and same-day avoidance, but should be watched because both can teach the same age-structure pattern.

New final-consumption-share is almost exactly the inverse of existing domestic-savings (Pearson r = -1.000 over 122 countries). It is practice-only and has same-day avoidance, but it is the clearest future retire/swap candidate if we want a stricter non-duplicate catalog.
Same trend-style emissions concept with different gases/aggregate; kept because they are expert/practice, not Daily, and answer different source series.
Same raw-count structure across taxa; kept expert-only because biodiversity geography is interesting but size/coverage can dominate.
Related natural-resource accounting measures; kept expert-only because they produce commodity-belt puzzles rather than default Daily maps.
Related trade-share concepts; kept because partner direction and service/merchandise scope make distinct spatial clues.
Related to existing account ownership/bank branches; kept outside Daily except the stronger account baseline already existed, with same-day avoidance present on the closest family.
Income-correlated, but kept because they are readable composite development/health puzzles with broad coverage; watch in playtests for too-obvious wealth gradients.
Inverse-adjacent energy/GDP concepts; kept as expert/practice rather than Daily because the units differ and patterns are useful but related.

boringGradientWatchlist.finding: These can read partly as “richer countries higher”; all still have policy/service-access patterns and sufficient coverage, but should be sampled during staging QA.
Not a rich-country gradient, but quite technical; kept expert-only to avoid dull Daily play.

duplicateAudit.conclusion: No exact duplicate IDs/provider codes/titles against the previous catalog. Several related families are intentionally kept at Practice or Expert-only; no clear removal recommended in this audit.

dailyManifestAudit.conclusion: The removed 2026-05-28 through 2026-06-01 routes are expected under the current 30-past/90-future rolling Daily manifest window. Dated routes are generated from dailies/index.json; preserving older routes would require an archive-retention policy change beyond this content batch.

pipelineGeodataAudit.buildPy: Intentional: CONTENT_VERSION changed from 2026.06.22-exp2-qa1 to 2026.07.02-exp4-content50 so generated artifacts and tests agree with this content batch.
pipelineGeodataAudit.worldGeojson: Intentional generated metadata churn only: contentVersion and Natural Earth retrievedAt changed; geometry/source checksum is unchanged.
